ground_truth.title: 3 Cheapest SEO Tools: My Honest Review of EACH One
ground_truth.author: Tiffani
ground_truth.main_content: I've been using Ubersuggest since I went full-time with my business in 2019. Lately? I can't stand it. So, what I've been doing the past few months is being a silly goose and using THREE different tools every time I do keyword research until I can finally decide which I like best based on price, accuracy, and overall how I like using it. A keyword research tool helps you figure out which topics are actually worth creating content for based on what people are already searching for. These tools give you a ton of data, but here are the only three metrics I really care about: Monthly Search Volume (how many people search for a keyword every month), Ranking Difficulty Score (stick with a ranking difficulty score under 50), and Intent (informational, commercial, or transactional). Ubersuggest was the cheapest SEO tool I could find when I first started this SEO biz, and it works. I used to LOVE Ubersuggest. What I like: Price is only $17 per month, User Experience is the prettiest and easiest to use, Uses Google's API, Email Updates, Site Audit, Keyword Ideas gives you hundreds of variations, Traffic Estimation for spying on competitors, and Answer The Public is included. What I don't like: Data is way off, you only get 200 credits to update data for free, ranking difficulty scores all seem to be over 50, and don't use Ubersuggest's AI Writer. Almost every SEO gal pal and website copywriter I know uses Keysearch as their primary keyword research tool. What I like: Price is only $17 per month, Easy to use, SEO for Social + Etsy, Rank Tracking just tells you everything you're ranking for. What I don't like: Doesn't Use Google's API, Data isn't accurate, Keyword Lists are frustrating to use, Deep Dive is annoying. SEMRush is the opposite of a cheap SEO tool, but they do have a FREE option which I've used for years just to double-check Ubersuggest's data. What I like: Accuracy, Detailed keyword suggestions and SERPs, Dashboard is easy to use. What I don't like: Price is over $100/month or $1K+ annually, Doesn't Use Google's API, Overwhelming to non-SEOers. Besides the free versions of the SEO tools above, Keywords Everywhere is going to be your cheapest option at only $21/year.
with: I've been using Ubersuggest since I went full-time with my business in 2019.
A keyword research tool helps you figure out which topics are actually worth creating content for based on what people are already searching for.
Ubersuggest was the cheapest SEO tool I could find when I first started this SEO biz, and lemme tell you—it works.
Almost every SEO gal pal and website copywriter I know uses Keysearch as their primary keyword research tool.
SEMRush is the opposite of a cheap SEO tool, but they do have a FREE option which I've used for years just to double-check Ubersuggest's data.
